Iraq Shampoo Dashboard

February 2026 | Iraq Shampoo Market

Executive InsightsMarketing TeamSales TeamTrade Marketing TeamVatika PotentialMarket OverviewBrand RankingManufacturersSummaryGeographyDaburDabur SKUVariantsDabur GeographyDabur ChannelsBrand RegionsBrand ChannelsBrand Sub-RegionsDistributionSegmentationSegmentationSegment — Brand Hi…Segment DetailBrand/SegmentPricingTier — Brand Hiera…Tier Deep DiveBrand/TierPack SizeSize Cross-tabBrand/Pack SizePromotionOrigin MixPositioningDist. EfficiencyGrowth DriversChannel PricingPromo Hierarchy

1. Executive Insights

MNCs dominate value (69%) but hold only 50% of volume — a 19pp premiumisation gap. Average MNC PPV is 10256 IQD/L vs market average of 7378 IQD/L.
Market contracted -2.9% in volume and -5.4% in value MoM (FEB vs JAN 2026), signalling seasonal softness or trade destocking.
IPEK is the biggest share gainer (+1.06pp volume), while Turkish brands collectively hold 15.7% volume at an average PPV of just 3775 IQD/L — the value disruptor segment.
Dabur (Vatika + Amla) holds 1.11% volume / 1.29% value with WD of only 9.8%. Distribution efficiency (13.2) suggests significant headroom — every 1pp WD gain could yield ~0.13pp value share.
HEAD&SHOULDERS leads distribution efficiency at 32.1 (Val%/WD), meaning it extracts more value per distribution point than any other top brand. SOFT WAVE is least efficient at 7.6.
Iraqi local brands hold 16.6% volume but only 7.2% value — competing purely on price (avg PPV 3216 IQD/L). They are vulnerable to any Turkish/Iranian brand offering similar pricing with better packaging.

2. Marketing Team

MARKETING: Brand Positioning & Portfolio Gaps
1. You're invisible in 5 of the 12 benefit segments — that's 1,416 K LTs of addressable market you're not playing in
Segment (Vatika absent)Seg Vol KLTSeg Val Mn IQDSegment LeaderLeader PPV IQDVatika PPVOpportunity
Damage Repair5633,561HAIR (Iraqi) — 17.4%2,7668,809Huge segment, leader is a local brand at 3× lower PPV. Vatika can enter with a repair variant at ~6,000 IQD/L and still command premium credentials. This is the biggest white space.
Nourishing3912,378SOFT WAVE (Lebanese) — 17.5%7,5798,809SOFT WAVE dominates with Hijab-positioned keratin variants. Vatika's natural oil heritage (coconut, argan) is a direct fit here. PPV gap is narrow — compete head-on.
Shine & Smoothing5012,598IPEK (Turkish) — 34.5%3,9108,809IPEK wins on price (PPV 3,910 vs Vatika 8,809). Entry here means a sharp price pack or a "premium smoothing" play that justifies the delta.
Normal / Daily2061,512PERT PLUS (MNC) — 10.9%7,8778,809Small gap. A daily-use Vatika variant at ~8,000 IQD/L fits perfectly. PERT PLUS is at 7,877 — Vatika can position as the natural daily alternative.
Kids & Baby2551,633NUNU (Saudi) — 42.1%6,311—NUNU owns this with 42% share and deep pharmacy distribution. Requires a dedicated kids SKU — not a variant stretch. Consider only if portfolio strategy supports it.
Action: Prioritise Damage Repair (563 KLT, weakest leader) and Nourishing (391 KLT, natural heritage fit) for next NPD cycle. Brief R&D on 2 SKUs targeting each segment.
2. Where you DO play, you're winning on value share efficiency — but volume share lags
Segment (Vatika present)Vatika Vol%Vatika Val%Val/Vol RatioImplication
Volume & Thickness5.0%8.6%1.72×Strongest premiumisation. Vatika commands 72% more value than its volume warrants. Protect this positioning.
Hair Fall Control3.5%4.1%1.17×Healthy premium. Hair Fall is a high-involvement category — Vatika's natural ingredient story resonates here.
Strengthening3.0%4.0%1.33×Good premiumisation. But LIFEBUOY (27% Vol share) is the gorilla here — focus on differentiation, not price war.
Dry & Frizz Control2.0%1.9%0.95×Slight value dilution. Garnier (29.6% Vol) owns this segment. Vatika needs a stronger frizz proposition or cede this to focus elsewhere.
Anti-Dandruff0.6%0.5%0.83×Below-parity. HEAD&SHOULDERS (56.8% Vol) is unassailable. Vatika's Anti-Dandruff SKU dilutes brand premium without gaining traction. Consider exit.
Action: Double down on Volume & Thickness and Hair Fall Control (high premiumisation). Review Anti-Dandruff SKU — it's diluting value share and competing against a 57% share gorilla.
3. Origin perception: Indian brands hold just 1.3% volume — but at PPV 8,810 (vs market avg 7,380). Your premium positioning is intact, the problem is awareness/reach, not brand equity.
OriginVol %Val %Avg PPV IQDvs Market PPVKey Insight
MNC49.9%69.4%10,256+39%Price leaders. But fragmented across many brands — no single MNC owns more than 17% Val.
Turkish15.7%8.0%3,775-49%Volume disruptors. IPEK+DURU grab share on pure price. They're your floor competitors — don't race to their level.
Iraqi16.6%7.2%3,216-56%Cheapest. HAIR + HAIR 2000 are pure commodity plays. Vatika should never compete here.
Indian1.3%1.6%8,810+19%Premium positioning intact. The gap between 1.3% volume and what comparable-PPV brands achieve (Garnier 4.8%, Lifebuoy 4.6%) shows the issue is distribution, not brand.
Action: Messaging should lean into "natural ingredients, Indian heritage" — this commands a premium. Don't dilute PPV to chase Turkish/Iraqi price points. The growth lever is distribution, not price.
4. Pack size architecture: 56% of the market is 400–700 ML. Vatika's 1.80% share here is its strongest size segment, but the 200–400 ML tier (trial/impulse) is nearly empty at 0.10%.
The 200–400 ML tier is 7.5% of volume but 11.4% of value — it's the highest-margin size segment in the market. Vatika holds only 0.10% volume share here. Every major competitor (H&S, Garnier, Pantene) has strong 200 ML trial packs driving first-purchase conversion. Vatika's absence in trial sizes is likely suppressing new user acquisition.

Action: Launch a 200 ML Vatika SKU as a trial/sampling pack at ~3,500 IQD PPU. This is not a margin play — it's a funnel play. The 400 ML repeat purchase is where the economics work.

3. Sales Team

SALES: Distribution Gaps & Territory Priorities
1. Kurdistan is a distribution desert — 0.16% volume share with WD 3.6. This is 843 K LTs of market you're barely in.
RegionMarket Vol KLTDabur Vol%Dabur Val%WDNDGap vs Baghdad WDPriority
Kurdistan8430.16%0.19%3.61.5-18.8 ppCRITICAL — Biggest gap
South1,0940.83%1.01%8.31.2-14.1 ppHIGH — Large market, low WD
Furat2790.92%1.09%15.02.6-7.4 ppMEDIUM
North7761.30%1.57%19.52.8-2.9 ppMEDIUM — Good efficiency
Baghdad1,7251.70%1.82%22.43.6—BENCHMARK
Action: Kurdistan should be the #1 territory priority. The region is 18% of Iraq shampoo volume. Even reaching Baghdad-level WD (22.4) would unlock ~1.4% share in a 843 KLT market = ~12 KLT incremental volume.
2. Sub-region drilldown: Kirkuk (WD 0.2) and Sulaymaniyah (WD 4.2) are near-zero despite being sizeable markets
Sub-RegionDabur Vol%WDNDStatusComparable Benchmark
Kirkuk0.07%0.21.4Near-zero presenceMusel (same region) has WD 35.3
Sulaymaniyah0.09%4.21.0NegligibleArbil (same region) has WD 5.3
Arbil0.35%5.33.3WeakBaghdad at WD 22.4
Other South0.45%7.61.0Below potentialBasra at WD 14.6
Musel2.01%35.310.2Star marketBest sub-region by far
Basra1.12%14.62.8SolidCan push to 20+
Action: Replicate the Musel playbook (WD 35.3, ND 10.2, Vol% 2.01) in Kirkuk and Sulaymaniyah. Whatever the Musel distributor is doing differently — territory coverage, retailer relationships, merchandising — document it and export it.
3. Distribution efficiency is 13.0 (Val%/WD×100) — comparable to DOVE (17.1) and better than VASELINE (5.0) or SOFT WAVE (7.6). Every point of WD you add delivers disproportionate returns.
BrandVal%WDEfficiencyWhat It Means
HEAD&SHOULDERS17.04%53.132.1Highest — but at WD 53 it's mature. Growth slows.
VATIKA1.27%9.813.0Strong efficiency at low WD = classic under-distributed brand. The S-curve has barely started.
GARNIER6.20%39.315.8Similar efficiency to Vatika at 4× the distribution. Shows what's possible.
SOFT WAVE3.66%48.47.6Over-distributed. 48% WD delivers only 3.7% value. Diminishing returns.
TRESEMME3.70%42.68.7Same story — high WD, low efficiency. Vatika extracts more per point of distribution.
Action: Present this efficiency data to the trade to negotiate better shelf space. Vatika delivers 13.0 value per WD point — better than Tresemme (8.7), Soft Wave (7.6), and NUNU (9.5). Retailers make more money per facings allocated to Vatika than to those brands.
4. Traditional Trade is the biggest channel gap: 0.34% Vol share with WD 1.2 in a 1,738 KLT market
ChannelMarket Vol KLTDabur Vol%Dabur Val%WDNDPPV IQDGap Assessment
Perfumery2,2191.59%1.53%16.87.89,078Best channel — push to WD 25
Modern Trade6851.56%1.85%13.67.68,055Good value premium. Negotiate end-caps.
Traditional Trade1,7380.34%0.54%1.20.68,497CRITICAL — Largest channel, near-zero presence. IPEK has WD 17.9, HAIR 12.9 here. Even WD 5 = significant gains.
Pharmacy770.48%0.46%3.31.39,930Small channel but highest PPV. Niche but worth maintaining.
Action: Traditional Trade is 37% of the total shampoo market. Vatika's WD of 1.2 here vs 16.8 in Perfumery represents the single largest distribution opportunity. Deploy van sales or wholesale distribution specifically for TT. Even reaching WD 5 in TT would roughly double Vatika's total Iraq volume.

4. Trade Marketing Team

TRADE MARKETING: Promotion, Pricing & Channel Architecture
1. Zero promoted volume for Dabur/Vatika. In a market where even P&G runs promos (Pantene 1.6%, H&S 0.5%), Vatika's complete absence from promotional activity is a missed lever.
BrandPromo Vol KLTTotal Vol KLTPromo %Promo Val Mn IQDPromo Tactic
TRICHUP (Indian)5.3997.26874.3%59.19Aggressive — 3/4 of volume is promoted. High trial-drive.
PANTENE (MNC)2.944180.9861.6%26.49Tactical — targeted promo without diluting base price.
HEAD&SHOULDERS (MNC)2.736519.2600.5%32.83Minimal — dominant brand barely needs it.
FREECIA (Chinese)2.0105.63735.7%21.00Heavy trial — new entrant buying share.
VATIKA (Indian)0.00051.6600.0%0.00ZERO. No trial-driving mechanism in market.
Action: Launch a targeted promo for Vatika in Perfumery channel (highest WD = 16.8) with a bundled sachet/trial offer. Even 2-3% promo intensity on 51.66 KLT base = ~1.5 KLT trial volume. In-store sampling at top 100 perfumeries would be more impactful than broad-base discounting. Do NOT run price promos — Vatika's premium positioning is an asset.
2. Channel pricing is inconsistent — Vatika's PPV ranges from 8,055 (Modern Trade) to 9,930 (Pharmacy). That's a 23% spread. Is this intentional or a sign of channel conflict?
ChannelVatika PPV IQDVatika PPV AEDMarket Avg PPVVatika PremiumKey Competitors in Channel
Perfumery9,07822.708,221+10%H&S (11,967), Elvive (13,330), Garnier (9,967) — premium company
Modern Trade8,05520.147,268+11%H&S (11,070), IPEK (3,772), HAIR (2,764) — wide price spread
Traditional Trade8,49721.246,393+33%IPEK (3,881), HAIR (2,782) — Vatika is 2.2× the avg here
Pharmacy9,93024.838,500+17%H&S (11,586), CLEAR (12,746), NUNU (6,762) — clinical positioning
Action: The +33% premium in Traditional Trade may be why WD is only 1.2 — retailers see it as too expensive for their shoppers. Consider a TT-specific pack (e.g., 200 ML at lower absolute price point, not lower PPV) to remove the sticker-shock barrier. The Modern Trade PPV of 8,055 is the most competitive — ensure MT is not cannibalising the Perfumery price architecture.
3. Perfumery is Vatika's strongest channel (WD 16.8) but is only 47% of Iraq shampoo. The other 53% is MT + TT + Pharmacy where Vatika barely exists.
Channel share of Dabur's total business vs channel share of market:
Perfumery = ~65% of Dabur volume but only 47% of market → over-indexed
Modern Trade = ~20% of Dabur but 15% of market → proportionate
Traditional Trade = ~12% of Dabur but 37% of market → massively under-indexed
Pharmacy = ~3% of Dabur but 2% of market → proportionate

The over-reliance on Perfumery creates concentration risk. If any major perfumery chain delists or negotiates harder, Dabur loses disproportionately.
Action: Develop a channel diversification KPI — target Traditional Trade contribution to reach 25% of Dabur volume within 12 months (from ~12% today). This requires: (a) smaller pack/lower unit price for TT, (b) wholesaler incentive scheme, (c) van sales coverage in top 3 TT-heavy regions (South, Baghdad, North).
4. Promotion geography: Basra (0.54%) and Musel (0.52%) have the highest promo penetration nationally. These are also Dabur's strongest sub-regions. Coincidence? No — these markets respond to activation.
Sub-RegionMarket Promo %Dabur Vol%Dabur WDInference
Musel0.52%2.01%35.3High promo activity + high Dabur share. Activation works here.
Basra0.54%1.12%14.6Same pattern — active market, good Dabur traction.
Kirkuk0.80%0.07%0.2Highest promo market but Dabur is absent. Competitors are spending here — Dabur is invisible.
Sulaymaniyah0.37%0.09%4.2Moderate promo. Dabur should enter with promotional support.
Arbil0.03%0.35%5.3Low-promo market. Distribution-led strategy more effective here.
Action: Kirkuk stands out — it has the highest promo penetration in Iraq (0.80%) meaning consumers here respond to promotions, yet Dabur has WD 0.2. Deploy a trade promo blitz in Kirkuk as a test case: 3-month distribution drive + in-store activation. If Kirkuk responds (like Musel), roll the playbook to Sulaymaniyah next.
5. Price tier reality check: Vatika sits in "Mainstream" (PPV ~9,000). The market's two biggest tiers are Value (1,315 KLT) and Premium (893 KLT). Mainstream (985 KLT) is the third tier. You're competing in the most crowded band.
Vatika at 4.2% share within Mainstream places it behind the tier's leaders but ahead of most. The strategic question: should Trade Marketing push Vatika UP into Premium (where margins are higher and Iraqi/Turkish brands don't compete) or hold Mainstream (where the volume pool is larger)?

Given Vatika's natural ingredients story and the 1.72× Val/Vol ratio in Volume & Thickness segment, the brand already behaves like a Premium product in segments where it wins. A selective price-up on hero SKUs (top 3 by volume) into the Premium band, while keeping a 400 ML "entry" SKU in Mainstream, would be the ideal architecture.
Action: Test a price-up of 8-10% on top-selling Vatika 400 ML variants (Egg Protein, Spanish Olive, Almond & Honey) in Perfumery only. Monitor WD and volume response over 2 months. If volume holds (which it should given the 1.72× premium already accepted in V&T segment), roll to MT.

5. Vatika Potential

What is Vatika worth if we close the distribution gap?
Current: 51.7 KLT volume · 455 Mn IQD (1.14 Mn AED) · WD 9.8 · ND 1.6
Methodology: Each region/channel has a measurable Vol-per-WD-point ratio. We project what happens if under-distributed territories reach benchmark levels. No PPV change assumed — pure distribution-led growth.
The Evidence: Vatika's distribution-to-share conversion is proven
Musel is the proof point. In Musel, Vatika has WD 35.3 (vs national avg 9.8) and achieves 2.01% volume share — nearly 2× the national average. This isn't a special market — it's what happens when distribution is properly executed. The model below uses each region's actual Vol/WD conversion rate, not theoretical assumptions.
RegionCurrent WDCurrent Vol KLTVol/WD RatioVal/WD Ratio (Mn IQD)Benchmark
Baghdad22.429.311.3111.51BENCHMARK — Best region
Kurdistan3.61.260.352.926.2× below Baghdad WD
North19.510.050.524.55Near benchmark — Musel star (WD 35.3)
South8.38.471.029.222.7× below Baghdad WD
Furat15.02.560.171.44Moderate gap
SCENARIO A: Geographic Distribution Expansion
Benchmark: Baghdad WD (22.4). Model: If under-distributed regions progressively close the gap with Baghdad, using each region's own Vol/WD and Val/WD conversion rates.
RegionMkt Vol KLTCurrent WD Conservative (50% of Baghdad WD = 11.2) Moderate (75% = 16.8) Aggressive (100% = 22.4)
Target WD+Vol KLT+Val Mn IQD+Val Mn AED Target WD+Vol KLT+Val Mn IQD+Val Mn AED Target WD+Vol KLT+Val Mn IQD+Val Mn AED
Kurdistan8433.6 11.2+2.7+22.1+0.055 16.8+4.6+38.4+0.096 22.4+6.6+54.6+0.137

North77619.5 ———— ———— 22.4+1.5+13.2+0.033
INCREMENTAL +5.6+48.8+0.122 +13.6+119.3+0.298 +23.7+208.4+0.521
NEW VATIKA TOTAL 57.3503.91.260 65.3574.41.436 75.4663.51.659
GROWTH vs TODAY +11% +26% +46%
SCENARIO B: Channel Distribution Expansion
Benchmark: Perfumery WD (16.8). Traditional Trade is the single biggest unlock — 37% of the shampoo market where Vatika has WD 1.2.
ChannelMkt Vol KLTCurrent WD Conservative (50% of Perf WD = 8.4) Moderate (75% = 12.6) Aggressive (100% = 16.8)
Target WD+Vol KLT+Val Mn IQD+Val Mn AED Target WD+Vol KLT+Val Mn IQD+Val Mn AED Target WD+Vol KLT+Val Mn IQD+Val Mn AED
Traditional Trade1,7381.2 8.4+35.5+301.5+0.754 12.6+56.2+477.4+1.193 16.8+77.0+653.2+1.633
Modern Trade68513.6 ———— ———— 16.8+2.4+19.4+0.049
Pharmacy773.3 8.4+0.6+5.7+0.014 12.6+1.0+10.4+0.026 16.8+1.5+15.0+0.038
INCREMENTAL +36.1+307.2+0.768 +57.3+487.7+1.219 +80.9+687.7+1.719
NEW VATIKA TOTAL 87.8762.31.906 109.0942.82.357 132.61,142.82.857
GROWTH vs TODAY +70% +111% +157%
Traditional Trade alone — even at conservative 50% of Perfumery WD (1.2 → 8.4) — delivers +35.5 KLT and +301.5 Mn IQD. That's more incremental value than the entire geographic expansion Scenario A at its most aggressive. Traditional Trade is the single highest-ROI distribution investment for Vatika in Iraq.
COMBINED REALISTIC SCENARIO: Geography + Channel (12-Month Target)
A blended, achievable 12-month plan combining moderate geographic expansion with focused Traditional Trade push.
LeverCurrent12-Month TargetWD Gain+Vol KLT+Val Mn IQD+Val Mn AEDFeasibility
Traditional Trade (national)WD 1.2WD 8.0+6.8 pp+33.6+285.0+0.713HIGH — Van sales + wholesale push. IPEK achieved WD 17.9 in TT.
Kurdistan (all channels)WD 3.6WD 12.0+8.4 pp+2.9+24.5+0.061HIGH — Currently near-zero. Distributor appointment needed.
South (all channels)WD 8.3WD 15.0+6.7 pp+6.8+61.7+0.154HIGH — Basra already at 14.6, replicate in Other South.
Perfumery (deepen)WD 16.8WD 22.0+5.2 pp+10.9+98.6+0.247MEDIUM — Already strongest, push into smaller perfumeries.
Modern Trade (deepen)WD 13.6WD 16.0+2.4 pp+1.8+14.6+0.036MEDIUM — Negotiate additional listings.
12-Month Incremental Volume
+56.0 KLT
51.7 → 107.7 KLT (+108%)

+484 Mn IQD
455 → 939 Mn IQD (+106%)
12-Month Value in AED
+1.21 Mn AED
1.14 → 2.35 Mn AED (+106%)
New Market Share Position
Volume share: 1.10% → 2.28% (ranks ~12th, overtaking ASSAYEL, SUNSILK)
Value share: 1.27% → 2.62% (ranks ~11th, overtaking NUNU, PERT PLUS)
At current PPV of 8,809 IQD/L — no price concession needed. Pure distribution play.
Why This Is Conservative
This model assumes zero market growth, zero PPV increase, and zero new SKUs. It also assumes linear Vol/WD relationship (reality: early WD gains are typically super-linear as you move from 0 → 10).
If you add NPD (Damage Repair, Nourishing variants) and TT-specific packs (200 ML), the upside could be 2–3× this estimate.
Implementation Priority Roadmap
QuarterActionTargetExpected Impact
Q1Appoint Kurdistan distributor · Launch TT wholesale scheme (Baghdad, South) · Deploy van sales in Basra/SouthKurdistan WD 3.6→8 · TT WD 1.2→4+15 KLT, +130 Mn IQD
Q2Expand TT to North (Musel model) · Deepen Perfumery in South · Launch 200 ML trial pack for TTTT WD 4→6 · Perfumery WD +3pp+18 KLT, +155 Mn IQD
Q3Kurdistan full rollout · TT push in Furat/Najaf · In-store activation in top 200 outletsKurdistan WD→12 · TT WD 6→7.5+13 KLT, +110 Mn IQD
Q4Consolidate gains · Push TT WD to 8 · MT listing expansion · Review and optimiseTT WD→8 · MT WD→16+10 KLT, +89 Mn IQD
